pilots.summary: Weak pipeline sanity baseline. The pilot proved bounded local mechanics but stored no replayable tensor checkpoint.
Replayable second small pilot with strong train loss decrease and finite dev/heldout loss, but a moderate train/dev and train/heldout gap.
Data-first balanced sampling improved dev and heldout loss over R25P while reducing the train/dev and train/heldout gaps.
Two-layer same-width architecture ablation improved train loss slightly versus R25S but worsened dev and heldout loss.

why_data_regularization: R25S improved dev and heldout behavior without increasing architecture depth.
R25V added depth and parameter count but worsened dev and heldout loss versus R25S.
R25Q/R25T weak buckets point to data balance, repeated templates, and regularization as safer phase_3 questions.
why_phase4_not_approved: Phase_3 pilots are still bounded mechanics and generalization checks, not product training.
The best heldout loss is still from a tiny pilot corpus and does not establish product capability.
Reviewer approval is required before any phase_4 scaled decoder training review can begin.
must_not_do: Do not run R25Y or any future pilot from an inert template.
Do not approve phase_4 scaled training in R25X.
Do not commit checkpoints, tokenizer artifacts, replay reports, or model-like binaries.
Do not introduce external models, LoRA, adapters, fine-tuning, backend inference, or external storage.
